name: List installed products given a Windows matlabroot path
description: Tests path inference, prodcontents.json reading, name cleanup, MATLABabetical sorting, and output format
prompt: What MATLAB products are installed at C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2025a?
expected_output: Agent reads prodcontents.json, derives Windows support package root, strips version and platform tokens from names, sorts MATLABabetically, and uses exact table format from skill

assertions.description: Agent reads prodcontents.json from the correct path
Agent derives the correct Windows support package root
Agent strips version and platform tokens from product names
Agent sorts products in MATLABabetical order
assertions.expected: MATLAB first, then MATLAB Copilot (if present), then Simulink (if present), then Simulink Copilot (if present), then alphabetical

assertions.description: Agent outputs using the exact table format from the skill
assertions.expected: Heading with release name, 'Installed at:' path, products table, support packages section

name: List installed products using release shorthand on Linux
description: Tests release shorthand expansion, Linux default path construction, and platform inference
prompt: Show me what's installed for 25a on Linux.
expected_output: Agent expands '25a' to 'R2025a', constructs /usr/local/MATLAB/R2025a path, derives Linux support package root, and outputs only the final formatted table

assertions.expected: R2025a in the resolved path and output heading

assertions.description: Agent constructs the correct Linux default matlabroot
Agent derives the correct Linux support package root
Agent executes silently and only shows the final formatted output
assertions.expected: No narration of intermediate steps — just the final table

name: Infer macOS platform from path and handle .app suffix
description: Tests macOS path format recognition, .app suffix stripping, and macOS support package root
expected_output: Agent infers macOS platform from path, strips .app suffix to extract R2025a, uses macOS support package root, and reads prodcontents.json from macOS matlabroot

assertions.description: Agent infers macOS from the /Applications/MATLAB_ path prefix
assertions.expected: Agent treats this as a macOS installation without asking for platform

assertions.description: Agent strips .app to extract release name R2025a
Agent uses the correct macOS support package root
Agent reads prodcontents.json from the macOS matlabroot
